claims.text: In Patient 1 (female), c.1935C>A (p.D645E) in exon 14 was identified as a heterozygous maternal allele classified pathogenic, in compound heterozygosity with c.1669A>T (p.I557F) from the paternal allele; she had symptom onset at 4 months, genetic diagnosis at 10 months, and died at 11 months with muscle weakness and cardiomegaly.

citations.quote:
1 | exon12 | c.1669A > T | p.I557F | het | paternal | pathogenic |
| | exon14 | c.1935C > A | p.D645E | het | maternal | pathogenic |

claims.text: Patient 8 (male) carried a homozygous c.1935C>A (p.D645E) mutation classified as pathogenic, with symptom onset at 1 month, diagnosed at 3 months, and died at 3 months with recurrent airway infections, cardiomegaly, and heart failure.

citations.quote: 8 | exon14 | c.1935C > A | p.D645E | hom | parental | pathogenic |
8 | M | 1 | 3 | 3 | N | Recurrent airway infections, cardiomegaly, heart failure

claims.text: The paper reports that c.1935C>A is the most common mutation in Thai patients with infantile-onset Pompe disease, accounting for 80% of the mutant alleles in that population.

citations.quote: In Thai patients, Amarinthnukrowh et al. reported that mutation c.1935C > A accounted for 80% of the mutant alleles and is the most common mutation in the Thai population (7).

claims.text: The paper states that the most common mutation in patients from southern China is c.1C>A (p.Asp645Glu), accounting for 20–25% of total mutant alleles, which by amino acid notation corresponds to p.D645E (likely a typographic error for c.1935C>A).

citations.quote: The most common mutation in patients from southern China was c.1C > A (p.Asp 645Glu), accounting for 20%–25% of the total mutant alleles (20).

claims.text: Reference 7 cited in the paper is specifically about p.D645E of acid α-glucosidase being the most common mutation in Thai patients with infantile-onset Pompe disease, confirming the identity of the c.1935C>A variant as p.D645E.

citations.quote: Amarinthnukrowh P, Tongkobpetch S, Kongpatanayothin A, Suphapeetiporn K, Shotelersuk V. P.D645E of acid α-glucosidase is the most common mutation in Thai patients with infantile-onset pompe disease. Genet Test Mol Biomarkers. (2010) 14(6):835–7.
